data.title: Trump's science funding cuts led to brain drain as US risks losing top scientists due to lower research budgets.
data.description: Budget cuts for research and criticism from the White House toward academia have created an opportunity for other nations to recruit top scientists. This shift has resulted in a significant increase in the number of leading researchers leaving the United States for overseas positions. The reduction in research funding and negative rhetoric from the White House have made academic environments in other countries more appealing, leading to a notable brain drain.

data.title: Spouse's unemployment: an obstacle. Should separation be considered?
data.description: In recent data, it was reported that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.2%, showing a decrease of 0.3% from the previous month. The total number of unemployed individuals decreased by 150,000, now totaling 6.7 million. Additionally, the employment rate saw a slight increase to 59.8%, with 32.5 million individuals now employed. The average hourly wage also rose by 2.5% compared to last year, now standing at $25.85. Overall, the labor market is showing signs of improvement with these recent statistics.

data.title: Federal deficit improves under Trump, but long-term outlook still challenging with potential budget implications.
data.description: Although there has been a decrease in the federal deficit under President Trump, his proposed plans may exacerbate the long-term financial situation. Currently, the federal deficit stands at approximately 3.9% of GDP, amounting to $779 billion. However, the President's policies, if implemented, could potentially lead to a significant increase in the deficit over the coming years, raising concerns about the sustainability of government finances in the long run.

data.title: Record number of student loan borrowers default on loans; many move abroad to escape debts.
data.description: An unprecedented number of borrowers with student loans are currently facing delinquency and default. The situation has driven some individuals to take extreme measures, such as leaving the country and abandoning their loans.
Rideshare giants give gas price help, but drivers feel it falls short of expectations.

data.title: Tens of millions of Americans are feeling the impact of Republican tax law as they file taxes this season.
data.description: The legislation approved by Republicans in the previous year has had minimal impact on the majority of Americans. However, the situation is evolving as millions of taxpayers are currently filing their taxes.

data.title: High fuel costs in Philippines prompt travelers to skip or limit Holy Week Visita Iglesia, a tradition of visiting seven churches.
data.description: Rising gasoline costs in the Philippines have led to individuals having to either cancel or reduce their participation in the Visita Iglesia, a traditional practice during Holy Week where Catholics visit seven churches.

data.title: Raw Dairy Farm recalls cheddar due to 9 E. Coli cases. Outbreak spans 3 states; producer denies involvement.
data.description: Raw Farm, a dairy producer based in California, is refuting claims that its products are responsible for the recent outbreak that has resulted in illnesses among individuals in three different states.

data.title: United to introduce no-frills fares in premium cabins, including premium economy and business class.
data.description: United Airlines has announced the implementation of new restrictive base fares in its premium economy and business class cabins. These changes aim to control costs and improve profitability for the airline. The introduction of these fares comes as United works to navigate ongoing challenges in the travel industry, such as fluctuating demand and rising operational expenses. This move is part of the company's strategy to optimize revenue and enhance its financial performance.

data.title: Big banks looking to participate in SpaceX’s IPO must buy subscriptions to Elon Musk’s Grok chatbot, a requirement for advising on one of the biggest IPOs ever.
data.description: Elon Musk is demanding that Wall Street companies buy subscriptions to his artificial intelligence chatbot if they wish to provide guidance on a groundbreaking initial public offering, which is set to be one of the largest in history.

data.title: Apple removes Bitchat app after China request. App used in protests in five countries. Launched in July.
data.description: Bitchat was introduced in July of the previous year and has been utilized in various protests across Madagascar, Uganda, Nepal, Indonesia, and Iran. Authorities in these countries have made efforts to limit internet access during these incidents.

data.title: Michael Saylor teases next Bitcoin buy strategy after pausing for a week; hints at future purchase on Sunday.
data.description: On Sunday, Michael Saylor made a post indicating a return to work on X, suggesting a possible Bitcoin acquisition. This move comes after the company halted purchases the previous week.

data.title: Trump's ultimatum spikes crypto values; hints at Iran deal nearing as he warns of consequences if Strait of Hormuz not opened.
data.description: The President of the United States, Donald Trump, issued a warning to Iran stating that the country could face dire consequences if it does not comply with opening the Strait of Hormuz. Trump suggested that Iran may be "living in Hell" if they fail to do so. However, in a separate statement to reporters, Trump mentioned that there is potential for a deal with Iran to be reached soon. The remarks come amid escalating tensions between the two countries.
All flights by Israeli carriers canceled until April 18, Israeli media say
JP Morgan hikes Merck & Co. price target to $135 from previous $125

data.title: Liven AS announces intention to conduct an initial public offering of shares and to apply for admission to trading on the Nasdaq Tallinn Stock Exchange Main List
data.description: Liven AS, known as Liven, plans to go public in the upcoming spring with an initial public offering of shares. The company aims to list its shares on the Nasdaq Tallinn Stock Exchange Main List. The offering will target retail and institutional investors in Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania. The capital raised from the share issuance will be allocated towards acquiring new development properties and executing the company's long-term growth strategy. Additionally, the move will assist in fortifying the group's capital structure for future endeavors.
